title: Human Vocal Fold System Architecture
brief: A technical engineering-style illustration showing human vocal fold anatomy from a systems engineering perspective. Cross-section diagram of the larynx with vocal cords, styled like a hardware engineering schematic with technical labels, measurement arrows, and annotations. Include: vocal fold length measurement, thickness specs, tension mechanism metaphor (springs), airflow dynamics arrows, and a small frequency response curve chart. Dark navy blue background with neon cyan and orange accent highlights. HUD-style callouts with bilingual Chinese/English labels. Title: 'FIGURE 2: 声带系统架构图 (Vocal Fold System Architecture)'. Mix medical illustration with cyberpunk engineering aesthetic. Clean lines, blueprint feel.
prompt:
Create a polished article illustration titled 'Human Vocal Fold System Architecture'. Diagram type: architecture diagram. Use a flat technical infographic illustration, clean grouped modules, clear arrows, soft professional palette, concise bilingual labels, document-friendly composition. Make it suitable for insertion into engineering notes or a design document. Prefer a clear information hierarchy with section titles, concise Chinese/English labels, short notes, arrows, grouped modules, and strong readability. Avoid dense paragraph blocks. Language mode: zh-en. Target aspect ratio preset: doc-2k. Mode: text-only. Content brief:
